entries.openingLines: It happened every year, was almost a ritual. And this was his eighty-second birthday. When, as usual, the flower was delivered, he took off the wrapping paper and then picked up the telephone to call Detective Superintendent Morell who, when he retired, had moved to Lake Siljan in Dalarna.

entries.openingLines: When I think of my wife, I always think of her head. The shape of it, to begin with—the V of her cheekbones going down to the point of her chin, the weight of her eyelids. I think of her earlobes, which are oddly satisfying, like the tip of a finger.
entries.title: Gone Girl
entries.author: Gillian Flynn

entries.openingLines: Renowned curator Jacques Saunière staggered through the vaulted archway of the museum's Grand Gallery and lunged for the nearest painting he could see, a Caravaggio.

entries.openingLines: Mma Ramotswe had a detective agency in Africa, at the foot of Kgale Hill. These were its assets: a tiny white van, two desks, two chairs, a telephone, and an old typewriter. Then there was a teapot, in which Mma Ramotswe – the only private lady detective in Botswana – brewed redbush tea. And three mugs – one for herself, one for her secretary, and one for the client.
entries.title: The No. 1 Ladies' Detective Agency
entries.author: Alexander McCall Smith

entries.openingLines: When Sean Devine and Jimmy Marcus were kids, their fathers worked together at the Coleman Candy plant and carried the scent of warm chocolate back home with them at the end of the day.

entries.openingLines: She's buried beneath a weeping willow tree, down towards the old train station, on the other side of the track. I used to watch for her, every morning and evening, and she never once looked up.
entries.title: The Girl on the Train

entries.openingLines: To Sherlock Holmes she is always the woman. I have seldom heard him mention her under any other name. In his eyes she eclipses and predominates the whole of her sex.

entries.openingLines: What I am telling you, before you begin my story, is this—two things: I crave truth. And I lie.

entries.openingLines: This is the story of what a Woman's patience can endure, and what a Man's resolution can achieve. From the devices of lawyers and the arts of others, the story is told in the words of the people who were actually present at the events described. The story begins at the point where the law and the post leave off.
entries.title: The Woman in White
entries.author: Wilkie Collins

entries.openingLines: Marsh is not swamp. Marsh is a space of light, where grass grows in water, and water flows into the sky.

entries.openingLines: The American handed Leamas another cup of coffee and said, "Why don't you go back and sleep? We can ring you if he comes."

entries.author: John le Carré

entries.openingLines: The trawler plunged into the angry swells of the dark, furious sea like an awkward animal trying desperately to break out of an impenetrable swamp. The waves rose to goliathan heights, crashing into the hull with the power of raw tonnage; the white sprays caught in the night sky cascaded downward over the deck under the force of the night wind. Everywhere there were the sounds of inanimate pain, wood straining against wood, ropes twisting, stretched to the breaking point. The animal was dying.
entries.title: The Bourne Identity
entries.author: Robert Ludlum

entries.openingLines: I haven't laid eyes on the island in several years. The last time was from a friend's boat that ventured into the outer harbor, and I could see it off in the distance, past the inner ring, shrouded in the summer haze, a careless smudge of paint against the sky.
entries.title: Shutter Island
entries.author: Dennis Lehane
